meta.disclaimer: Reasoned analytical estimates from live team-news research, not live bookmaker prices. Fun-money only — always check the actual odds before you stake.
meta.method: Per-match deep research on both squads (probable XIs, form, injuries, set-piece + penalty takers) via live web search, refreshed daily and again near kickoff when lineups are confirmed.

win.reason: Nations League champions with a top-tier midfield face a DR Congo side set up to frustrate and counter.

scorers.note: Leads the line at his 6th World Cup; 28 goals in 30 club games and Portugal's penalty/free-kick man.
Creative hub and secondary penalty option; arrives late into the box.
Most explosive wide threat; one moment of pace settles a low-block game.
Drifts inside off the right to finish in tight spaces against a packed defence.

assists.note: Chief chance creator from open play and set-pieces; main supply to Ronaldo.
Controls tempo from deep, threading line-breaking passes through the block.
Marauding left-back overlapping Leão for cutbacks.

penalty.note: Territorial dominance vs a deep block invites box contact; Ronaldo is the designated taker (~85% career).

lineups.home: Diogo Costa; Cancelo, Rúben Dias, Inácio, Nuno Mendes; João Neves, Vitinha; Bernardo Silva, Bruno Fernandes, Leão; Ronaldo
lineups.away: Mpasi; Tuanzebe, Mbemba, Kapuadi; Wan-Bissaka, Sadiki, Moutoussamy, Mukau, Masuaku; Wissa, Bakambu

playerNotes.note: Central striker at 41 in a record 6th World Cup; 28 goals in 30 for Al Nassr, primary penalty + free-kick taker.
Creative engine and tempo-setter, leads chance creation; secondary penalty option behind Ronaldo.
Most explosive wide outlet; the pace-and-power profile that unlocks a deep DR Congo block.
Newcastle forward, the Leopards' main counter threat on the left; carries pace to hurt Portugal in transition.
Captain and most-capped player (107+ caps) marshalling the back three; their organisation hinges on him.
Veteran striker and record scorer (21 intl goals); holds the ball up, though club form has dipped.

win.reason: Unbeaten in last 3 vs Croatia, 0 conceded in 8 qualifiers; Croatia shipped 10 in their last 6.

scorers.note: Captain and spot-kick taker; 61 goals in 2025-26 for Bayern, Golden Boot favourite.
Advanced No.10 with late runs into the box; second goal threat from midfield.
Favoured to start left wing; direct runner to exploit Croatia's leaky back line.
Cuts inside off the right despite a managed Achilles issue.

assists.note: Right-side chance creator; deliveries and cutbacks for Kane and Bellingham.
Links midfield to Kane; through-balls and set-up runs.
Right-back overlaps and crossing; key set-piece delivery.

penalty.note: Croatia's stretched, error-prone defence concedes regularly; Kane is automatic from the spot.

lineups.home: Pickford; James, Stones, Guéhi, O'Reilly; Anderson, Rice; Saka, Bellingham, Gordon; Kane (4-2-3-1)
lineups.away: Livaković; Stanišić, Vušković, Gvardiol; Sutalo, Modrić, Kovačić, Perišić; Sučić, Baturina; Musa

playerNotes.note: Talismanic captain and penalty taker; a Europe-leading 61 goals in 2025-26, the clear focal point and Golden Boot favourite.
Central creator/runner behind Kane; secondary goal + assist threat with late box arrivals.
Right-wing matchwinner managing a minor Achilles issue; cuts in to shoot and supplies cutbacks.
40-year-old captain at a record 6th World Cup (now AC Milan); still the metronome but legs short for 90 high-tempo minutes.
Lone striker, in good MLS form (12 in 13) but isolated against a settled England backline.
Veteran wide threat after 26 goal involvements for PSV; Croatia's main outlet and aerial set-piece danger.

win.reason: Personnel edge through Semenyo and Jordan Ayew despite missing Kudus and Partey; Panama lack playmaker Carrasquilla — take Ghana with the draw insured in a tight game.

scorers.note: Captain, penalty taker and qualifying top scorer (7); leads the line and is Ghana's likeliest source with Kudus out.
Ghana's most in-form attacker after a strong PL season; pace and end product from wide.
Panama's qualifying top scorer (8, 2 pens) and penalty taker; dangerous on the counter.
Physical striker with 4 qualifying goals; aerial set-piece threat in a low-scoring game.

assists.note: Primary wide creator for Ghana; most probable assist provider with Kudus absent.
Drops to link play and takes set pieces; creates as well as finishes.
Panama's chief creator on transitions with Carrasquilla missing.

penalty.note: Both sides defend deep and physically; a spot-kick from a congested box is plausible. Ayew takes for Ghana, Díaz for Panama.

lineups.home: Ati-Zigi; Senaya, Adjetey, Opoku, Mensah; Owusu, Yirenkyi, Nuamah; Boakye, Ayew, Semenyo — no Kudus (injury), Salisu (ACL), Partey (visa)
lineups.away: Mosquera; Ramos, Córdoba, Andrade; Murillo, Godoy, Harvey, Davis; Rodríguez, Díaz, Fajardo — Carrasquilla a doubt

playerNotes.note: Captain and talisman at his 3rd World Cup; 7 in qualifying, the penalty/set-piece taker and focal point with Kudus ruled out.
Most in-form Ghanaian after a standout PL campaign; pace and directness make him the biggest open-play danger.
Lyon attacker adding creativity and carrying through midfield, filling the void left by Kudus and Partey.
León forward and Panama's qualifying top scorer (8); dynamic on the counter, the primary threat and penalty taker.
2024 CONCACAF Player of the Year and key playmaker, but a muscular injury makes him a doubt — a major blow to Panama's ball progression.
Experienced (68 caps) physical striker; leads the press and offers an aerial set-piece threat.

win.reason: Superior individual quality, 4 wins in last 5 (15 scored, 4 conceded); Uzbekistan debutants missing creator Masharipov.

scorers.note: Primary threat; 7 goals + 3 assists in qualifying, relentless runner who exploits the spaces wide of a back three.
Lone striker and central focal point with Durán dropped; poacher's instinct for second-half chances at altitude.
Orchestrator and penalty/free-kick taker; can score from range or the spot.
Impact sub in the final 30 as Colombia chase a late goal.

assists.note: CONMEBOL-high 6 assists and 61 key passes in qualifying; chief creator to break the low block.
3 assists in qualifying; cutbacks from the left for Suárez.
Overlapping right-back supplying crosses against a deep side.

penalty.note: Heavy possession vs a deep block invites box congestion; James scored two penalties in qualifying.

lineups.home: Yusupov; Ashurmatov, Khusanov, Abdullaev; Nasrullayev, Hamrobekov, Shukurov, Sayfiyev; Fayzullaev, Urunov; Shomurodov (3-4-2-1)
lineups.away: Vargas; Muñoz, Sánchez, Lucumí, Mojica; Lerma, Ríos; Arias, Rodríguez, Díaz; Suárez (4-2-3-1)

playerNotes.note: Talisman left winger in excellent form (7 goals, 3 assists in qualifying); incessant running and 1v1 quality is Colombia's most reliable route.
34-year-old captain and playmaker, CONMEBOL's top creator in qualifying (6 assists); primary set-piece and penalty taker.
Lone striker with Durán dropped after a fall-out with Lorenzo; the central finisher feeding off Díaz and James.
Captain and all-time top scorer (44 in 90 caps); carries the entire counter-attacking threat as lone forward.
Manchester City centre-back, the squad's standout talent; anchors an organised back three that must absorb pressure.
Creative spark and set-piece taker; carries the bulk of chance-creation with Masharipov a doubt.
